name: Hilbert–Arnold Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The phase space of an integrable Hamiltonian system, closed under symplectic structure and bounded by the energy surface

VINIT.justification: The void state before perturbation — a completely integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with no external disturbance, the genesis of the theorem's domain

TANCH.justification: The closed hermetic vessel: the phase space M with its symplectic form ω, which contains and anchors all dynamics — the constitutional court of allowed trajectories

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation from integrable (ε=0) toward perturbed (ε>0), growth of deviation from integrability

AREV.justification: Reverse/descent: the contravariant return path, recovering the integrable structure as perturbation vanishes, descent back to unperturbed state

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: the canonical transformation (p,q) → (I,φ) composed with the perturbation flow, root→derived form of the phase space

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity/self-reference: the action I is self-recognizing — it is the quantity that remains invariant under slow perturbation, the stone that knows itself

FSPLIT.chosen_element: separation into resonant and non-resonant tori
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: the perturbed phase space branches into two distinct regions — resonant tori (where ω·k=0 for integer k) and non-resonant tori (generic Diophantine condition)

FFUSE.chosen_element: KAM theorem reconstruction of invariant tori
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (resonant and non-resonant) reconstitute the original perturbed phase space structure — FFUSE(FSPLIT(phase space)) = perturbed phase space with most tori preserved

EVALT.justification: Affirmative/success: the KAM theorem affirms that a non-resonant torus with Diophantine frequency satisfies the persistence condition — it is preserved under small perturbation

EVALF.justification: Negative/failure: resonant tori fail the KAM condition — they are destroyed or undergo bifurcation, creating chaotic regions and lower-dimensional remnants

ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: at the critical perturbation strength, a torus is simultaneously preserved and destroyed — held in both states without resolution, the albedo stage of phase transition

IFIX.chosen_element: measure of destroyed phase space volume
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: the permanent record of which tori were destroyed — the Lebesgue measure of the resonant region is irreversibly fixed once perturbation is applied, append-only destruction log

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: separation into resonant and non-resonant tori
phase_2.split_input: perturbed Hamiltonian phase space with small ε
split_outputs: non-resonant tori (Diophantine frequencies, measure-preserving under KAM)
resonant tori (rational frequency ratios, destroyed or bifurcated)
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: KAM reconstruction of the perturbed phase space
phase_2.fuse_result: the full perturbed phase space with most (measure-theoretically full) tori preserved and resonant regions destroyed — semantically equals the original perturbed system

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the unperturbed integrable system H₀, before any perturbation ε is applied; no distinction between resonant and non-resonant regions yet exists
phase_3.true_description: 01 — a non-resonant torus persists under perturbation; the KAM condition is satisfied; the torus is preserved with action variables I slowly varying
phase_3.false_description: 10 — a resonant torus is destroyed or bifurcates; the KAM condition fails; the torus dissolves into chaotic layers and lower-dimensional remnants
phase_3.both_description: 11 — the critical resonance boundary where a torus is simultaneously preserved and destroyed; the paradice of phase transition; both KAM success and failure held without resolution

steps.domain_action: genesis of the unperturbed integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with action-angle variables (I, φ) — the void state before perturbation
the symplectic manifold M with symplectic form ω closes and contains all allowed phase space trajectories — the hermetic boundary
perturbation parameter ε increases from 0 — forward morphism toward the perturbed regime H = H₀ + εH₁
composition of the canonical transformation (p,q) → (I,φ) with the perturbed Hamiltonian flow — chaining of morphisms
action variables I are identified as adiabatic invariants — self-recognizing quantities that remain slowly varying under perturbation
the perturbed phase space branches into two regions: non-resonant tori (generic Diophantine frequencies ω·k ≠ 0) and resonant tori (rational frequencies ω·k = 0)
non-resonant torus branch — the KAM theorem affirms persistence; the torus survives with action I slowly varying, measure-preserving dynamics
resonant torus branch — the KAM condition fails; resonant tori are destroyed, bifurcate, or dissolve into chaotic layers
critical resonance boundary at ε = ε_c — a torus is simultaneously preserved and destroyed, held in paradice without resolution
adiabatic limit ε → 0 — reverse morphism, descent back toward the integrable structure as perturbation vanishes
KAM reconstruction — the two branches (non-resonant preserved, resonant destroyed) reconstitute the full perturbed phase space; FFUSE(FSPLIT(H)) = H_perturbed
the measure of destroyed resonant phase space is permanently recorded — irreversible fixation of which tori were destroyed, append-only destruction log
the action variables I close the loop — self-reference confirms that adiabatic invariants are the canonical coordinates for understanding persistence
composition of all transformations — the full cycle of perturbation, branching, reconstruction, and return to adiabatic limit
the symplectic manifold boundary is reaffirmed — all dynamics remain within the closed phase space structure

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Perturbation theory and KAM analysis — translates the Hamiltonian H = H₀ + εH₁ and frequency conditions into persistence/destruction verdicts for each torus
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Symplectic form ω — the canonical communication protocol between action and angle variables; Poisson brackets {·,·} mediate all interactions
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Action-angle variables (I, φ) — state is stored in the adiabatic invariants I (slowly varying) and angles φ (fast rotating); the phase space itself is the memory substrate
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Hamiltonian flow — the time evolution is determined by ∂I/∂t = -∂H/∂φ and ∂φ/∂t = ∂H/∂I; resonance conditions ω·k = 0 determine which tori are processed first
phase_5.alfs_store: The integrable Hamiltonian H₀ and its action-angle representation — the canonical reference and bootstrap store for all perturbative analysis

phase_6.cycle_cost: Measure of destroyed phase space volume — each perturbation cycle consumes the Lebesgue measure of resonant tori; entropy increases via phase space contraction
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unperturbed integrable system with full measure of tori; all action variables I are conserved; phase space is foliated by invariant tori
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Perturbed system with measure-theoretically full set of surviving non-resonant tori; resonant tori destroyed; action variables I slowly drift; chaotic layers appear
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative in the measure-theoretic sense: the KAM theorem preserves a set of full measure (Lebesgue measure of surviving tori → full measure as ε → 0), so the total phase space volume is conserved by Liouville's theorem; destroyed resonant tori have measure zero in the limit

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → AREV → FFUSE → IFIX → IMSCRIB → CLINK → TANCH
-- Class: Hilbert–Arnold Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑱  | identity — self-imscription
--   [13] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑠 → 𐑡  | composition — regime coherence
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑱 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def hilbert_arnold_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (genesis of the unperturbed integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with action-angle variab...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the symplectic manifold M with symplectic form ω closes and contains all allo...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (perturbation parameter ε increases from 0 — forward morphism toward the pertu...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of the canonical transformation (p,q) → (I,φ) with the perturbed ...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (action variables I are identified as adiabatic invariants — self-recognizing ...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (the perturbed phase space branches into two regions: non-resonant tori (generic Diophantine frequencies ω·k ≠ 0) and resonant tori (rational frequencies ω·k = 0)) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (non-resonant torus branch — the KAM theorem affirms persistence; the torus su...)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (resonant torus branch — the KAM condition fails; resonant tori are destroyed,...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (critical resonance boundary at ε = ε_c — a torus is simultaneously preserved ...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (adiabatic limit ε → 0 — reverse morphism, descent back toward the integrable ...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (KAM reconstruction — the two branches (non-resonant preserved, resonant destroyed) reconstitute the full perturbed phase space; FFUSE(FSPLIT(H)) = H_perturbed)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (the measure of destroyed resonant phase space is permanently recorded — irrev...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑱)  -- [12] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the action variables I close the loop — self-reference confirms that adiabati...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of all transformations — the full cycle of perturbation, branchin...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the symplectic manifold boundary is reaffirmed — all dynamics remain within t...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def hilbert_arnold_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (hilbert_arnold_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def hilbert_arnold_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (hilbert_arnold_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem hilbert_arnold_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
